userStories.title: Initialize React Router 7 project with core dependencies
userStories.description: Create the new React app inside a `medici-app/` directory at the project root. Steps: (1) Run `npx create-react-router@latest medici-app` (accept defaults, TypeScript). (2) `cd medici-app && npm install`. (3) Install additional deps: `npm install zustand lucide-react`. (4) Verify `npm run dev` starts without errors — visit the default React Router welcome page. (5) Commit all generated files. IMPORTANT: The medici-app/ directory is the NEW codebase. The existing vanilla files (index.html, js/, css/) stay untouched — they are the reference implementation. Do NOT delete or modify them.

userStories.title: Install and configure Tailwind CSS v4
userStories.description: Inside medici-app/: (1) Run `npm install tailwindcss @tailwindcss/vite`. (2) Add the Tailwind Vite plugin to vite.config.ts: import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite' and add tailwindcss() to plugins array. (3) In the root CSS file (app/app.css or equivalent), add `@import 'tailwindcss';` at the top. (4) Verify `npm run dev` still works and Tailwind classes are applied (test by adding a `className='text-red-500'` to any element and confirming it renders red). (5) Remove the test class after verification. No tailwind.config.js needed in v4.

userStories.title: Install and configure shadcn/ui + install all needed components
userStories.description: Inside medici-app/: (1) Run `npx shadcn@latest init` — the CLI auto-detects React Router 7. Accept defaults. This creates components.json, components/ui/, and lib/utils.ts with cn() helper. (2) Install all needed shadcn components in batches: `npx shadcn@latest add button card dialog dropdown-menu input label` then `npx shadcn@latest add tabs table badge separator scroll-area` then `npx shadcn@latest add tooltip popover sheet avatar switch slider` then `npx shadcn@latest add command sidebar skeleton alert`. (3) Verify `npm run dev` still starts without errors. (4) Verify that importing a component like Button from '~/components/ui/button' works by temporarily rendering one. VERIFICATION: ls components/ui/ should show 20+ component files. `npm run dev` loads cleanly.

userStories.title: Port design tokens to shadcn CSS variables + set up fonts
userStories.description: Inside medici-app/: (1) Copy fonts/ChicagoFLF.ttf to public/fonts/ChicagoFLF.ttf. (2) In app/app.css (after the tailwind import), add @font-face for ChicagoFLF and @theme block defining custom font families: --font-pixel: 'ChicagoFLF', monospace; --font-mono: 'IBM Plex Mono', monospace; --font-sans: 'DM Sans', sans-serif. (3) Add @layer base with :root {} containing ALL shadcn CSS variables mapped from the existing tokens.css. Map every color: --background maps to --off-white (#FAF9F7 → HSL), --foreground to --taupe-5, --primary to --violet-3, --secondary to --taupe-1, --muted-foreground to --taupe-3, --destructive to --red, --border to --taupe-2. Also add Medici custom extensions: --amber, --green, --blue, --berry, --violet-1 through --violet-4, plus all -rgb triplet tokens. (4) Add .dark {} section with ALL dark mode remappings from tokens.css [data-theme='dark']. REFERENCE: Read the existing css/tokens.css carefully — every :root variable and every [data-theme='dark'] override must be ported. (5) Add Google Fonts import for IBM Plex Mono and DM Sans (or use @fontsource packages). VERIFICATION: `npm run dev` loads. Inspect element shows CSS variables on :root. Both light theme vars and .dark vars are present in the stylesheet.

userStories.title: Create project directory structure with placeholder files
userStories.description: Inside medici-app/app/, create the full directory tree from the refactoring plan. Create directories: routes/, components/layout/, components/chat/, components/workflows/, components/brain/, lib/, services/, data/, stores/, hooks/. Create placeholder files (empty or with a single export comment) for every file listed in the plan's section 1.6. Specifically: (1) routes/: _app.tsx, _app.chat.tsx, _app.chat.$threadId.tsx, _app.workflows.tsx, _app.workflows.$id.tsx, _app.brain.tsx, _app.brain.memory.tsx, _app.brain.lessons.tsx, _app.brain.lessons.$id.tsx, _app.brain.graph.tsx, login.tsx. (2) components/layout/: app-sidebar.tsx, app-header.tsx, cosimo-panel.tsx, logo.tsx. (3) components/chat/: thread-list.tsx, message-block.tsx, message-stream.tsx, chat-input.tsx, file-panel.tsx, workflow-panel.tsx, artifact.tsx, data-table.tsx, gate-message.tsx. (4) components/workflows/: workflow-card.tsx, workflow-stats.tsx, template-detail.tsx, flow-graph.tsx, flow-node.tsx, flow-edge.tsx, node-popover.tsx, overview-tab.tsx, schema-tab.tsx, triggers-tab.tsx, runs-tab.tsx, lessons-tab.tsx. (5) components/brain/: memory-list.tsx, memory-fact.tsx, trait-badges.tsx, lesson-card.tsx, lesson-detail.tsx, entity-graph.tsx, entity-detail.tsx. (6) lib/: icons.ts, color-utils.ts (utils.ts already exists from shadcn). (7) services/: types.ts, threads.ts, workflows.ts, brain.ts, panels.ts. (8) data/: mock-threads.ts, mock-workflows.ts, mock-brain.ts, mock-panels.ts, config.ts. (9) stores/: ui-store.ts, chat-store.ts, workflow-store.ts, brain-store.ts, theme-store.ts. (10) hooks/: use-theme.ts, use-keyboard.ts, use-resize-panel.ts, use-command-autocomplete.ts. Each placeholder file should contain: `// TODO: Implement [filename description]\nexport {};` so TypeScript doesn't complain about empty modules. VERIFICATION: `find app -name '*.ts' -o -name '*.tsx' | wc -l` shows 55+ files. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes (no type errors from placeholders).

userStories.title: Define all TypeScript interfaces in services/types.ts
userStories.description: Create comprehensive TypeScript interfaces in medici-app/app/services/types.ts covering every data shape in the existing mock-data.js. Read js/mock-data.js from the vanilla prototype carefully. Define: (1) Thread interface: id, title, preview, timestamp, model, workflowRunId?, indicators (ThreadIndicator[]), messages (Message[]). (2) ThreadIndicator: type ('ready'|'waiting'|'running'|'streaming'), label. (3) Message: id, type ('user'|'ai'), content (string), model?, timestamp?, attachments (Attachment[])?, artifacts (Artifact[])?, isGate?, gateStatus?, commandChip?. (4) Attachment: name, type, size?, pages?, sheets?. (5) Artifact: title, type ('table'|'flow-graph'|'metadata'|'text'), data. (6) WorkflowTemplate: id, title, description, status ('draft'|'active'|'paused'|'archived'), version, createdBy, createdDate, triggerType (TriggerType), triggerConfig, linkedLessons (string[]), linkedEntities (string[]), inputSchema (InputSchema), outputSchema (OutputSchema), nodes (FlowNode[]), edges (FlowEdge[]), runs (RunStats), recentRuns (RecentRun[]). (7) TriggerType: 'manual'|'folder-watch'|'schedule'|'email'|'chat-command'|'chained'. (8) TriggerConfig: watchPath?, chatCommand?, schedule?, emailAddress?. (9) FlowNode: id, type ('input'|'action'|'gate'|'branch'|'output'), title, description, lesson (string|null), x (number), y (number), conditions? (BranchCondition[]). (10) BranchCondition: label, target. (11) FlowEdge: from, to, label?. (12) RunStats: total, successRate, avgDuration, filesProcessed. (13) RecentRun: id, status ('success'|'failed'), trigger, time, duration, threadId (string|null). (14) WorkflowRun: templateId, runId, status ('running'|'waiting'|'completed'|'failed'), triggerType, triggeredBy, startTime, threadId, inputManifest (FileManifest[]), nodeStatuses (Record<string, NodeStatus>), exceptions (RunException[]), outputManifest (FileManifest[]). (15) NodeStatus type: 'pending'|'running'|'completed'|'failed'|'skipped'|'waiting'. (16) RunException: nodeId, type ('inference'|'low-confidence'|'conflicting-value'|'format-unknown'), description, confidence (number|null). (17) FileManifest: name, type?, size?, path?, fileCount?, pages?, sheets?. (18) InputSchema: description, fields (SchemaField[]). (19) SchemaField: name, type ('string'|'number'|'date'|'currency'|'enum'|'boolean'), required, description, options?. (20) OutputSchema: format, destination, columns. (21) WorkflowCommand: command, label, description, templateId. (22) MemoryData: role, personalityTraits (PersonalityTrait[]), facts (MemoryFact[]). (23) MemoryFact: text, category, linkedEntities? ({name, type}[]). (24) PersonalityTrait: name, active. (25) Lesson: id, title, scope ('user'|'company'), lastUpdated, content, linkedWorkflows. (26) GraphData: nodes (GraphNode[]), edges (GraphEdge[]). (27) GraphNode: id, label, type ('person'|'fund'|'property'|'document'|'company'), group. (28) GraphEdge: source, target, label. (29) CalendarData, TaskData, UsageData, SpreadsheetData interfaces matching MOCK_CALENDAR, MOCK_TASKS, MOCK_USAGE, MOCK_SPREADSHEET shapes. VERIFICATION: `npx tsc --noEmit` passes with no errors on this file. Every type used in mock-data.js has a corresponding interface.

userStories.title: Create data/mock-threads.ts — extract all chat thread data
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/data/mock-threads.ts. Read the existing index.html to extract all 8 chat thread message scripts as structured data using the Thread/Message interfaces from types.ts. The 8 threads are: (1) 'fund3' — Fund III Rebalance (5 messages with artifacts, user file attachments, wash sale data table). (2) 'hilgard' — Hilgard Capital Analysis (messages about fund analysis with spreadsheet artifact). (3) 'q4lp' — Q4 LP Reporting (messages about LP distributions). (4) 'k1' — K-1 Processing (messages with K-1 extraction results, data table, retry button). (5) 'erabor' — The Erabor Partnership (streaming demo thread with multi-section response, thinking animation). (6) 'wf-run-rentroll-047' — Rent Roll Extraction Q4 Batch (workflow run thread — /rent-roll command chip, 3 PDF attachments, run artifact, spreadsheet preview artifact per CLAUDE.md Thread A script). (7) 'tener-batch-12' — Tener Valuation Filing Batch 12 (workflow gate thread — /valuation command chip, 47 files, exception table, gate messages per CLAUDE.md Thread B script). (8) 'wf-create-dd' — New Workflow: Due Diligence (creation flow per CLAUDE.md Thread C script). For each thread, extract: id, title, preview (first ~60 chars of first AI message), timestamp, model, indicators array, workflowRunId (if applicable), and messages array with full content, attachments, artifacts, gate status. For artifact data tables, structure the data as arrays of objects. Export as `export const MOCK_THREADS: Record<string, Thread>`. Also export `export const MOCK_THREAD_ORDER: string[]` with the thread IDs in sidebar display order. CRITICAL: Read the mock conversation scripts in the project CLAUDE.md for threads 6-8 — they have exact message content specified. VERIFICATION: Import the file and check that `Object.keys(MOCK_THREADS).length === 8`. Each thread has a non-empty messages array. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

userStories.title: Create data/mock-workflows.ts — templates, runs, commands
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/data/mock-workflows.ts. Read the existing js/mock-data.js to extract MOCK_WORKFLOW_TEMPLATES, MOCK_WORKFLOW_RUNS, and MOCK_WORKFLOW_COMMANDS. Also read the Template Data Model and Run Data Model sections of the project CLAUDE.md for the complete data specs. Export three objects: (1) `MOCK_WORKFLOW_TEMPLATES: Record<string, WorkflowTemplate>` with all 7 templates: rent-roll, k1-extract, lp-waterfall, fee-calc, covenant, tener-valuation, due-diligence. Each must have complete data: id, title, description, status, version, createdBy, createdDate, triggerType, triggerConfig, linkedLessons, linkedEntities, inputSchema (with all fields), outputSchema, nodes array (with x/y positions, types, descriptions, conditions for branches), edges array (with labels for branches), runs stats, recentRuns. (2) `MOCK_WORKFLOW_RUNS: Record<string, WorkflowRun>` with 2 runs: wf-run-rentroll-047 (completed, 3 input PDFs, all nodes completed, gate skipped, 1 inference exception at 94% confidence, 1 output xlsx) and wf-run-tener-12 (waiting, 47 input files, gate node waiting, 3 exceptions: conflicting values, poor OCR, unexpected TIFF). (3) `MOCK_WORKFLOW_COMMANDS: WorkflowCommand[]` with 5 commands: /rent-roll, /k1, /fees, /valuation, /dd. VERIFICATION: Template count is 7, run count is 2, command count is 5. Each template's nodes and edges can be traversed from input to output. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

userStories.title: Create data/mock-brain.ts — memory, lessons, graph data
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/data/mock-brain.ts. Read the existing js/mock-data.js to extract MOCK_MEMORY, MOCK_LESSONS, and MOCK_GRAPH_DATA. Export: (1) `MOCK_MEMORY: MemoryData` with role string, personalityTraits array (each with name + active boolean), and facts array (each with text, category from 'preference'|'workflow'|'contact'|'fund'|'style'|'context', and linkedEntities array with {name, type} pairs). Port ALL existing facts and traits from mock-data.js. (2) `MOCK_LESSONS: Record<string, Lesson>` with ALL existing lessons. Each has id, title, scope ('user'|'company'), lastUpdated, content (full markdown text), linkedWorkflows array. (3) `MOCK_GRAPH_DATA: GraphData` with nodes array (each: id, label, type, group) and edges array (each: source, target, label). Port ALL existing graph nodes and edges. VERIFICATION: Fact count matches original mock-data.js. Lesson count matches. Graph node/edge counts match. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

userStories.title: Create data/mock-panels.ts + data/config.ts
userStories.description: Create two files in medici-app/app/data/: (1) mock-panels.ts — export MOCK_TASKS (task items with title, due, status, priority), MOCK_CALENDAR (month string, events array with title/meta/color), MOCK_USAGE (stats for tokens, cost, model breakdown), MOCK_SPREADSHEET (column headers + row data for the Fund III file panel demo). Port ALL data from existing js/mock-data.js. (2) config.ts — export a CONFIG object with all extracted constants: fontZoomLevels ([1, 1.05, 1.1, 1.15, 1.2]), memoryCategories, lessonScopes, sidebar dimensions (narrowSnap: 56, narrowThreshold: 110), graphAnimMs (500), eraborTimingMs (2000), flowGraph dimensions (nodeWidth: 160, nodeHeight: 60, compactNodeWidth: 100, compactNodeHeight: 36, colSpacing: 200, rowSpacing: 100, edgeStroke: 1.5). Also extract CONFIG_PURPLE_BASE_COLORS from mock-data.js and any other CONFIG_* objects. Use `as const` assertions for type safety. VERIFICATION: `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. All CONFIG values are typed and exported.

userStories.title: Create all service modules
userStories.description: Create 4 service files in medici-app/app/services/ that wrap data access behind async functions. (1) threads.ts: getThreads() returns all threads as array, getThread(id) returns single thread or null, getThreadOrder() returns MOCK_THREAD_ORDER. (2) workflows.ts: getTemplates() returns all templates as array, getTemplate(id) returns single or null, getRun(id) returns run or null, getRunsForTemplate(templateId) filters runs, getCommands() returns MOCK_WORKFLOW_COMMANDS. (3) brain.ts: getMemory() returns MOCK_MEMORY, getLessons() returns all lessons as array, getLesson(id) returns single, getGraphData() returns MOCK_GRAPH_DATA. (4) panels.ts: getTasks() returns MOCK_TASKS, getCalendar() returns MOCK_CALENDAR, getUsage() returns MOCK_USAGE, getSpreadsheet() returns MOCK_SPREADSHEET. Each function is async (returns Promise) even though it returns mock data synchronously — this ensures the API contract is ready for real backend calls. Import types from './types' and data from '~/data/*'. VERIFICATION: `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. Each service function has correct return types.

userStories.title: Create lib/icons.ts + lib/color-utils.ts
userStories.description: Create 2 utility files: (1) medici-app/app/lib/icons.ts — Create a mapping object that maps the old ICONS keys to lucide-react icon components. Read js/icons.js from the vanilla prototype to get all icon names used. Map each to the closest lucide-react equivalent. Example mappings: person→User, building→Building2, folder→Folder, lessons→BookOpen, settings→Settings, search→Search, plus→Plus, chevronLeft→ChevronLeft, chevronRight→ChevronRight, folderWatch→FolderSearch, schedule→Clock, chatCommand→Terminal, emailTrigger→Mail, manualTrigger→Play, webhook→Link. Export the mapping as `export const iconMap: Record<string, LucideIcon>`. Also export a helper `getIcon(name: string): LucideIcon | undefined`. (2) medici-app/app/lib/color-utils.ts — Port the ColorUtils namespace from app.js: hexToHsl(hex), hslToHex(h,s,l), hexToRgbString(hex), adjustPurpleIntensity(baseColors, intensity). These are used by the purple intensity slider. Type all params and returns. VERIFICATION: `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. Both files export correctly typed functions/objects.

userStories.title: Create all Zustand stores
userStories.description: Create 5 Zustand stores in medici-app/app/stores/: (1) theme-store.ts — useThemeStore with: theme ('light'|'dark'), purpleIntensity (number 0-100), fontSizeLevel (number 0-4), dyslexiaFont (boolean), reducedMotion (boolean), highContrast (boolean), iconLabels (boolean). Actions: toggleTheme, setPurpleIntensity, cycleFontSize, toggleDyslexiaFont, toggleReducedMotion, toggleHighContrast, toggleIconLabels. Wrap with zustand/middleware persist({name:'medici-theme'}) for localStorage persistence. (2) ui-store.ts — useUIStore with: currentMode ('chat'|'workflows'|'brain'), sidebarCollapsed (boolean), cosimoPanelOpen (boolean), taskPanelOpen (boolean), calendarPanelOpen (boolean), usagePanelOpen (boolean), profileMenuOpen (boolean), profileSubpanelOpen (boolean). Actions: setMode, toggleSidebar, openCosimoPanel, closeCosimoPanel, closeAllPanels, toggleTaskPanel, toggleCalendarPanel, toggleUsagePanel, toggleProfileMenu, showProfileSubpanel, showProfileMain. (3) chat-store.ts — useChatStore with: activeThreadId (string|null), filePanelOpen (boolean), workflowPanelOpen (boolean), activeWorkflowRunId (string|null), autocompleteOpen (boolean), autocompleteIndex (number), isStreaming (boolean). Actions: selectThread, openFilePanel, closeFilePanel, openWorkflowPanel, closeWorkflowPanel, showAutocomplete, hideAutocomplete, setAutocompleteIndex, setStreaming. (4) workflow-store.ts — useWorkflowStore with: activeTemplateId (string|null), activeTab (string default 'overview'), selectedNodeId (string|null), popoverOpen (boolean), graphZoom (number), graphPan ({x,y}). Actions: selectTemplate, setTab, selectNode, openPopover, closePopover, setGraphZoom, setGraphPan. (5) brain-store.ts — useBrainStore with: activeBrainSection ('memory'|'lessons'|'graphs'), memoryCategory (string), lessonScope (string), activeLessonId (string|null), graphSelectedEntity (string|null). Actions: setBrainSection, setMemoryCategory, setLessonScope, setActiveLessonId, selectGraphEntity. VERIFICATION: `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. Each store can be imported and its initial state accessed.

userStories.title: TypeScript compilation checkpoint — fix all type errors
userStories.description: This is a VERIFICATION TASK. Run `cd medici-app && npx tsc --noEmit` and fix every type error. Common issues to check: (1) Import paths using ~ alias — verify tsconfig paths are configured. (2) Missing type exports or circular dependencies between types.ts and data files. (3) Mock data objects not matching their declared types (extra fields, missing required fields, wrong types). (4) Service functions returning wrong types. (5) Zustand store types not matching their implementations. Fix all errors. Then run `npm run dev` and verify the app loads (even though it's mostly empty). VERIFICATION: `npx tsc --noEmit` exits with code 0. `npm run dev` starts without build errors.

userStories.title: Create root.tsx — HTML shell, theme provider, font loading
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/root.tsx (or update the existing one from create-react-router). This is the outermost layout. It should: (1) Import useThemeStore and read theme, dyslexiaFont, reducedMotion, highContrast, fontSizeLevel. (2) Import CONFIG for fontZoomLevels. (3) Render <html> with: lang='en', className={theme === 'dark' ? 'dark' : ''}, data-a11y-font, data-a11y-motion, data-a11y-contrast attributes based on store state, and inline style for fontSize based on fontSizeLevel. (4) Include <head> with meta tags, Google Fonts links for IBM Plex Mono and DM Sans, and Links/Meta from React Router. (5) Include <body> with <Outlet /> and <ScrollRestoration />. (6) Import app.css. (7) The theme store uses persist middleware so it auto-reads from localStorage on load. VERIFICATION: `npm run dev` renders. Toggle between light/dark by directly setting localStorage and reloading — HTML element should get .dark class. Inspect element shows data-a11y attributes when set.

userStories.title: Create _app.tsx layout + configure routes.ts with full route tree
userStories.description:
Create medici-app/app/routes/_app.tsx as the app shell layout AND configure app/routes.ts with the complete route tree. PART A — routes.ts: Create the route configuration using React Router 7's route() and layout() helpers. Register ALL routes even though most are placeholders: (1) route('login', 'routes/login.tsx'). (2) layout('routes/_app.tsx', [...]) containing: route('chat', 'routes/_app.chat.tsx') with child route(':threadId', 'routes/_app.chat.$threadId.tsx'), route('workflows', 'routes/_app.workflows.tsx') with child route(':id', 'routes/_app.workflows.$id.tsx'), layout('routes/_app.brain.tsx', [route('brain/memory', 'routes/_app.brain.memory.tsx'), route('brain/lessons', 'routes/_app.brain.lessons.tsx') with child route(':id', 'routes/_app.brain.lessons.$id.tsx'), route('brain/graph', 'routes/_app.brain.graph.tsx')]). (3) Add an index redirect from '/' to '/chat'. PART B — _app.tsx: Create the app shell layout wrapping all authenticated routes. Structure: (1) A flex-row container (full viewport height). (2) Left: component (placeholder for now — just render a div with 'Sidebar' text, width 240px, border-right). (3) Right: flex-col with (placeholder — just render a div with 'Header' text, height 48px, border-bottom) and a main content area with . (4) Import useUIStore for sidebarCollapsed state. When collapsed, sidebar div should be 48px wide. (5) Add the Cosimo panel placeholder (a shadcn Sheet component from the right side, controlled by cosimoPanelOpen from UIStore). (6) Use Tailwind classes for layout: flex, h-screen, overflow-hidden, etc. PART C — Update placeholder route files: Ensure every route file in routes/ exports a minimal default component (e.g., `export default function ChatRoute() { return
Chat View
; }`) so routes.ts doesn't error on missing exports. VERIFICATION: `npm run dev` starts. Navigate to /chat, /workflows, /brain/memory, /brain/lessons, /brain/graph — each shows its placeholder text. / redirects to /chat. No 404s for any route.

userStories.title: Create logo.tsx — 6-sphere logo with exact CSS preservation
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/components/layout/logo.tsx. This is one of the few components that preserves exact custom CSS rather than using shadcn. Read the existing index.html and css/layout.css to extract the logo markup and styles. (1) Create a LogoMark component (small sidebar version): renders .logo-mark > .logo-mark-inner containing 6 .logo-sphere divs arranged in an inverted triangle (3 top, 2 middle, 1 bottom). Each sphere has .dark and .light half-spheres with radial gradients. (2) Create a LogoFull component (login page version): renders .hero-spheres with 6 larger spheres. (3) Add all logo CSS as a CSS module or inline Tailwind + custom CSS. The sphere gradients use: radial-gradient(circle at 35% 30%, var(--violet-3) 0%, var(--violet-4) 100%) for dark halves, radial-gradient(circle at 65% 30%, var(--violet-1) 0%, var(--violet-2) 100%) for light halves. The inverted triangle layout uses CSS grid or flex. (4) Include the drift animation (gentle floating) gated by [data-a11y-motion='reduce']. VERIFICATION: Import LogoMark in the sidebar placeholder. The 6 spheres render in the correct inverted triangle layout. Dark mode shows appropriate color shifts.

userStories.title: Create app-sidebar.tsx — full sidebar with shadcn Sidebar
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/components/layout/app-sidebar.tsx using the shadcn Sidebar component. Read the existing index.html sidebar HTML and app.js sidebar logic. Structure: (1) Top: .logo-row with LogoMark component + 'COSIMO' title + 'MEDICI & COMPANY' subtitle. Add sidebar-toggle-btn (ChevronLeft/Right) that calls UIStore.toggleSidebar(). (2) Below logo: search input (shadcn Input) for global thread search. (3) '+ New' button (shadcn Button). (4) Thread list section: render ThreadList component (placeholder for now — will be built in R-24). Show all threads from MOCK_THREAD_ORDER. Active runs section at top with tinted background for workflow threads that are running/waiting. (5) Brain nav section at bottom: 3 buttons (Memory, Lessons, Graphs) that navigate to /brain/*. Include collapse toggle (chevron) that calls UIStore.toggleBrainNav(). (6) Handle collapsed state: when sidebarCollapsed, show only logo spheres + toggle button (width 48px). (7) Store sidebarCollapsed in localStorage (already in theme-store or ui-store). (8) Tailwind + minimal custom CSS for the retro bevel border on the sidebar edge. VERIFICATION: Sidebar renders with logo, search, thread list area, brain nav. Collapse toggle hides content and narrows sidebar. Dark mode works.

userStories.title: Create app-header.tsx — top bar with tabs and action icons
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/components/layout/app-header.tsx. Read existing index.html header and app.js UI namespace. Structure: (1) Left section: mode tabs — Chat, Workflows, Brain. These are navigation links (React Router <Link>) to /chat, /workflows, /brain/memory. Active tab has underline/highlight styling. Use ChicagoFLF font for tab labels. (2) Right section: icon buttons for Tasks (CheckSquare icon), Calendar (Calendar icon), Usage (BarChart3 icon). Each toggles its respective panel via UIStore (toggleTaskPanel, etc.). (3) Rightmost: Profile avatar button (circle with 'E' initial) that toggles profile dropdown. (4) All icon buttons use shadcn Button variant='ghost' with lucide-react icons. (5) Use Tailwind for layout: flex justify-between, items-center, h-12, border-b. (6) The header panels (tasks, calendar, usage) are rendered as shadcn Popover/DropdownMenu components positioned below their trigger buttons. For now, render placeholder content in each panel — the full content comes later. VERIFICATION: Header renders with 3 mode tabs, 3 action icons, profile avatar. Tab clicks navigate to correct routes. Dark mode works.

userStories.title: App shell checkpoint — verify shell renders correctly
userStories.description: This is a VERIFICATION TASK. Verify the complete app shell: (1) `npm run dev` starts without errors. (2) Navigate to /chat — app shell shows sidebar (with logo, thread area, brain nav) + header (with tabs, icons, avatar) + empty main content. (3) Navigate to /workflows — header 'Workflows' tab is active. (4) Navigate to /brain/memory — header 'Brain' tab is active. (5) Click sidebar collapse toggle — sidebar narrows to 48px, only spheres visible. (6) Click again — sidebar expands. (7) Toggle dark mode (via theme store or temporarily with a button) — entire shell adapts. (8) No console errors. (9) `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. Fix any issues found. IMPORTANT: If the app shell has bugs, ALL subsequent component work will be built on a broken foundation. Be thorough.

userStories.title: Create message-block.tsx — user and AI message rendering
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/components/chat/message-block.tsx. This renders a single message — either user or AI. Read the existing index.html message HTML patterns and chat.css. (1) User message: .msg-block containing .user-card with avatar (circle with initial 'E'), username, timestamp, and .msg-body with message text content. If message has commandChip, render it as a styled span (font-mono, primary bg, white text, rounded pill). If message has attachments, render .msg-file-attachments with file cards (icon, name, meta). (2) AI message: .msg-block containing .ai-block with Cosimo avatar (sphere icon or 'C'), model badge (e.g. 'Cosimo 2.1'), timestamp, .msg-body with content. If message has artifacts, render each using the Artifact component (placeholder import for now). (3) Gate message variant: if message.isGate, add amber left border, amber background tint, '⏸ Awaiting Review' badge chip. Use shadcn Badge, Avatar components where appropriate. Tailwind for layout. VERIFICATION: Component renders both user and AI message types without errors when given sample message data. TypeScript compiles.

userStories.title: Create artifact.tsx + data-table.tsx for inline artifacts
userStories.description: Create two components: (1) medici-app/app/components/chat/artifact.tsx — renders an inline artifact within a message. Structure: .artifact container with .artifact-header (icon + title + expand/collapse button) and .artifact-body. Based on artifact.type: 'metadata' renders key-value pairs, 'table' renders DataTable component, 'flow-graph' renders a placeholder for the flow graph (will be wired later), 'text' renders formatted text content. Use shadcn Card for the container styling. (2) medici-app/app/components/chat/data-table.tsx — renders a data table inside an artifact. Uses shadcn Table, TableHeader, TableRow, TableHead, TableBody, TableCell. Accepts columns (string[]) and rows (Record<string, string|number>[]). Styles: monospace font for data cells, alternating row shading, horizontal scroll on overflow. Both components need dark mode support via Tailwind dark: variants. VERIFICATION: Components render sample data without errors. Table displays columns and rows correctly. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

userStories.title: Create chat-input.tsx — rich text input area
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/components/chat/chat-input.tsx. Read the existing index.html input area HTML and app.js Chat namespace for the input logic. Structure: (1) Container .input-area: optional .input-file-strip at top (for staged file chips — render if stagedFiles prop is non-empty), then .input-row containing: text input (shadcn Input or textarea, auto-grows), attach button (Paperclip icon with dropdown menu: 'From computer' + 'From cloud drive'), send button (ArrowUp icon, shadcn Button). (2) Below input-row: .input-footer with model selector dropdown (shadcn DropdownMenu showing model name like 'Cosimo 2.1 ▾') and keyboard shortcut hint text ('↵ to send, Shift+↵ for new line'). (3) The input should detect '/' as first character and show autocomplete dropdown (will be wired via hook later — for now just accept an onSlashCommand callback prop). (4) File chip strip: each staged file shows icon (by type), truncated filename, size, and × remove button. Horizontally scrollable. (5) Props: onSend(text), onAttach(type), stagedFiles[], modelName, onModelChange. VERIFICATION: Component renders with input, send button, attach menu, model selector. Typing works. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

userStories.title: Create thread-list.tsx — sidebar thread items with workflow indicators
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/components/chat/thread-list.tsx. This renders the thread list in the sidebar. Read existing index.html thread items and app.js Chat.selectThread. (1) Accept threads (Thread[]) and activeThreadId props. (2) Split threads into two groups: 'Active Runs' (threads with workflowRunId where the linked run status is 'running' or 'waiting') and regular threads. (3) Active Runs section: tinted background, section header. Each item shows template name, status indicator (amber dot + 'Waiting' or violet dot + pulse for 'running'). (4) Regular threads: each item is a shadcn SidebarMenuButton (or styled button) showing thread title, preview text (truncated), timestamp, and indicator badge if present. Workflow-linked threads show a small gear icon (⚙). (5) Active thread gets highlighted background. (6) Click navigates to /chat/{threadId} using React Router Link or useNavigate. (7) Pulse animation for 'running' indicator, gated by data-a11y-motion via Tailwind. VERIFICATION: Thread list renders with sample thread data. Active thread highlighted. Workflow indicators visible. Click navigates. Dark mode works.

userStories.title: Create _app.chat.tsx + _app.chat.$threadId.tsx routes
userStories.description: Create two route files: (1) medici-app/app/routes/_app.chat.tsx — The chat layout route. Loader calls getThreads() and getThreadOrder(). Renders: sidebar thread list (ThreadList component with threads data) and an Outlet for the selected thread content. If no thread is selected (index route), show a centered placeholder: 'Select a thread or start a new conversation'. (2) medici-app/app/routes/_app.chat.$threadId.tsx — The thread detail route. Loader: calls getThread(params.threadId), throws 404 if not found. Also calls getRun(thread.workflowRunId) if the thread has one. Renders: scrollable message area with MessageBlock for each message in thread.messages, ChatInput at the bottom, and optionally the WorkflowPanel (if run exists) or FilePanel. Add ErrorBoundary that shows 'Thread not found' using shadcn Alert. VERIFICATION: Navigate to /chat — thread list appears. Click a thread — messages render. Unknown thread ID shows error boundary. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

userStories.title: Chat view checkpoint — verify full chat functionality
userStories.description: This is a VERIFICATION TASK. Thoroughly verify the chat view: (1) Navigate to /chat — thread list renders all 8 threads in correct order. (2) Click 'Fund III' thread — messages render with user cards, AI blocks, artifacts, data tables. (3) Click 'Rent Roll Extraction' thread — /rent-roll command chip visible, PDF attachments listed, run artifact shows metadata, spreadsheet preview table renders. (4) Click 'Tener Valuation' thread — gate messages have amber styling, '⏸ Awaiting Review' badge visible, exception table renders. (5) Click 'New Workflow: Due Diligence' thread — creation conversation renders normally. (6) Chat input renders with text area, send button, attach menu, model selector. (7) Active thread highlighted in sidebar. (8) Dark mode: repeat checks 2-4 in dark mode. (9) No console errors. (10) `npx tsc --noEmit` passes. Fix ALL issues found before proceeding.

userStories.title: Create workflow-card.tsx + workflow-stats.tsx
userStories.description: Create two components: (1) medici-app/app/components/workflows/workflow-card.tsx — A library card for a workflow template. Uses shadcn Card, CardHeader, CardContent, CardFooter, Badge, Button. Structure: header has title (font-mono, semi-bold) + status Badge (variant by status: active=default, draft=secondary, paused=outline, archived=secondary) + trigger type chip (small pill with icon from iconMap + trigger type label). Body: description text. Footer: linked lesson chip if any (violet pill with ◆ + lesson name), run stats ('47 runs · 95.7% success'), and '▶ Run' Button (small, outline variant, visible on card hover). Props: template (WorkflowTemplate), onSelect(id), onRun(id). (2) medici-app/app/components/workflows/workflow-stats.tsx — Stats bar above the library grid. Shows 4 stat boxes: Total Templates (count), Active (filtered count), Total Runs (sum of all template runs.total), Success Rate (weighted average). Use Tailwind grid layout. VERIFICATION: Both components render with sample template data. Card shows all fields. Stats calculate correctly. Dark mode works.

userStories.title: Create flow-graph.tsx + flow-node.tsx + flow-edge.tsx — SVG flow graph
userStories.description: Create three SVG components for the flow graph: (1) medici-app/app/components/workflows/flow-graph.tsx — Container component. Props: nodes (FlowNode[]), edges (FlowEdge[]), compact? (boolean), nodeStatuses? (Record<string, NodeStatus>), selectedNodeId?, onNodeSelect?(id). Calculates viewBox from node positions using CONFIG.flowGraph dimensions. Full mode: nodeWidth=160, nodeHeight=60, colSpacing=200, rowSpacing=100. Compact mode: 100×36, colSpacing=130, rowSpacing=65. Renders SVG with <defs> for arrowhead marker, then FlowEdgeComponent for each edge, then FlowNodeComponent for each node (nodes on top of edges). (2) flow-node.tsx — Renders a single node. Props: node, status?, selected?, compact?, config, onClick. Renders: <g> with <rect> (rounded corners via rx/ry = 6px), <text> for title (mono 11px bold), <text> for description (sans 10px, only in full mode). Colors by node type: input=blue stroke/fill, action=violet, gate=amber dashed, branch=taupe, output=green. If status provided, override fill color: completed=green, running=violet+pulse, waiting=amber, pending=faded taupe, failed=red, skipped=faded+strikethrough. Gate nodes use strokeDasharray='6 3'. Selected node gets a highlight ring. (3) flow-edge.tsx — Renders edge line between two nodes. Props: edge, nodes (for position lookup), config. Calculates start point (bottom center of 'from' node) and end point (top center of 'to' node). Renders <line> or <path> with arrowhead marker-end. Branch edges show label at midpoint. Stroke color: taupe-3, 1.5px. VERIFICATION: Render the rent-roll template's nodes/edges — graph should show input→action→branch→gate→action→action→output flow. Nodes have correct colors by type. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

userStories.title: Create node-popover.tsx — node detail popover on graph click
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/components/workflows/node-popover.tsx using shadcn Popover. Props: node (FlowNode), templateId (string), open (boolean), onClose(), anchorPosition ({x, y}). Structure: (1) PopoverContent (320px wide): type label Badge at top (colored by node type), title in mono font, full description text, linked lesson detail (if node.lesson is set — show lesson name as a link), 'Edit with Cosimo' Button that calls UIStore.openCosimoPanel(), 'Close' Button. (2) Position the popover near the clicked node using the anchorPosition prop. (3) Click-outside-to-dismiss via Popover's built-in behavior. (4) Tailwind + dark mode support. VERIFICATION: Component renders with sample node data. Shows correct type badge color. Close button works. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

userStories.title: Create template-detail.tsx container with shadcn Tabs
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/components/workflows/template-detail.tsx — the main container for viewing a workflow template's detail. Uses shadcn Tabs. Props: template (WorkflowTemplate), run? (WorkflowRun). Structure: (1) Header row: back button (ChevronLeft + 'Back to Library' — navigates to /workflows), template title, status Badge, actions DropdownMenu ('Edit with Cosimo', 'Duplicate', 'Archive'), '▶ Run' Button. (2) Two-column layout below header: left column (60%) contains the FlowGraph component rendering the template's nodes/edges; right column (40%) contains Tabs: Overview, Schema, Triggers, Runs, Lessons. (3) Each tab renders its respective component (placeholder imports for now). (4) The flow graph column has a subtle bevel border and overflow hidden. (5) Node clicks in the graph open the NodePopover. Wire: onNodeSelect sets WorkflowStore.selectNode, which opens the popover positioned near the node. VERIFICATION: Component renders with sample template. Two-column layout displays. Tabs switch content. Graph renders in left column. Dark mode works.

userStories.title: Create overview-tab.tsx + schema-tab.tsx
userStories.description: Create two tab content components: (1) medici-app/app/components/workflows/overview-tab.tsx — Props: template (WorkflowTemplate). Renders: description section, metadata grid (version, created by, created date, trigger type with icon), linked entities list (if any), linked lessons list (if any). Use Tailwind for a clean two-column metadata layout. (2) medici-app/app/components/workflows/schema-tab.tsx — Props: template (WorkflowTemplate). Renders: Input Schema section (description text + field table using shadcn Table — each row: field name in mono bold, type Badge chip, required indicator star, description text). Output Schema section (format chip, destination path, column list as comma-separated or badges). The type Badge uses different colors per type: string=taupe, number=blue, date=green, currency=amber, enum=violet, boolean=berry. VERIFICATION: Both components render with rent-roll template data (which has the most complete input schema). Table shows all 8 input fields. Output shows xlsx format. Dark mode works.

userStories.title: Create triggers-tab.tsx + runs-tab.tsx + lessons-tab.tsx
userStories.description: Create three tab content components: (1) triggers-tab.tsx — Props: template. Lists all active triggers: icon by type (from iconMap), type label, config detail (folder path, cron expression, command string). 'Add Trigger' dashed-border button at bottom. (2) runs-tab.tsx — Props: template. Run history table using shadcn Table. Columns: status dot (colored), run ID, trigger + detail, timestamp, duration. Rows with threadId get cursor-pointer and hover effect — clicking navigates to /chat/{threadId} via useNavigate(). Show template.recentRuns data. (3) lessons-tab.tsx — Props: template. Card for each linked lesson (cross-reference template.linkedLessons with lesson data). Each card: lesson name, which nodes reference it, last updated, link to /brain/lessons/{id}. 'Link Lesson' dashed-border button. VERIFICATION: All 3 tabs render with rent-roll template data. Runs tab shows 3+ recent runs. Trigger tab shows folder-watch + chat-command triggers. Lessons tab shows linked lesson. Dark mode works.

userStories.title: Create _app.workflows.tsx + _app.workflows.$id.tsx routes
userStories.description: Create two route files: (1) medici-app/app/routes/_app.workflows.tsx — The workflow library route. Loader calls getTemplates(). Renders: WorkflowStats bar at top, then a grid of WorkflowCard components (3 columns). Card click navigates to /workflows/{id}. Run button on card triggers a toast for now ('Run functionality coming soon'). Also renders sidebar content via Workflows.renderSidebar pattern: active runs section + template list. (2) medici-app/app/routes/_app.workflows.$id.tsx — Template detail route. Loader: calls getTemplate(params.id), throws 404 if not found. Also calls getRunsForTemplate(id). Renders TemplateDetail component with template + run data. Add ErrorBoundary. VERIFICATION: Navigate to /workflows — 7 template cards visible in grid, stats bar shows correct totals. Click a card — detail view opens with flow graph + tabs. Back button returns to library. Dark mode works. `npx tsc --noEmit` passes.

userStories.title: Create lesson-card.tsx + lesson-detail.tsx

Renders: shadcn Card with title, scope Badge ('user'=violet bg white text, 'company'=blue bg white text), last updated date, truncated content preview, linked workflows count. Click calls onSelect. VERIFICATION: Card renders with sample lesson data. Detail shows full content. Edit mode toggles. Scope badges have correct colors. Dark mode works.

userStories.title: Create entity-graph.tsx + entity-detail.tsx
userStories.description: Create two Brain graph components: (1) entity-graph.tsx — Props: graphData (GraphData), selectedEntityId?, onSelectEntity(id). This is an SVG force-directed graph (simplified version of the original). Read the existing Graph namespace in app.js. Render nodes as circles (colored by type: person=blue, fund=violet, property=green, document=amber, company=taupe) with labels. Render edges as lines with labels. Selected node is enlarged/highlighted. Provide zoom/pan via SVG viewBox manipulation (mouse wheel + drag). For the mock implementation, use a static layout (pre-computed positions from the original) rather than implementing force-directed simulation — this keeps the task atomic. (2) entity-detail.tsx — Props: entity (GraphNode), relatedEdges (GraphEdge[]), onNavigate(id). Renders: entity name, type Badge, list of related entities (connected via edges) as clickable items. Each related entity click calls onNavigate. VERIFICATION: Graph renders with MOCK_GRAPH_DATA. Nodes colored by type. Click a node → entity detail panel appears. Dark mode works.

userStories.description: Create 5 route files: (1) _app.brain.tsx — Brain layout route. Renders section navigation (Memory, Lessons, Graphs tabs — these are links to /brain/memory, /brain/lessons, /brain/graph) and <Outlet />. The navigation tabs should highlight the active section based on the current URL. (2) _app.brain.memory.tsx — Loader: calls getMemory(). Renders MemoryList component. (3) _app.brain.lessons.tsx — Loader: calls getLessons(). Renders lesson list with LessonCard components. Provides <Outlet /> for lesson detail. userStories.title: Create header dropdown panels — tasks, calendar, usage
userStories.description: Create or flesh out the header dropdown panels: (1) In app-header.tsx, replace placeholder panels with full implementations. Tasks panel: Uses shadcn Popover. Calendar shows correct March 2026 layout with day 14 highlighted. Usage shows stats. Panels close on outside click. Dark mode works.

Workflow panel opens for workflow-linked threads showing compact graph with status colors. Both panels close correctly. Dark mode works.

userStories.title: Create use-command-autocomplete.ts hook + wire into chat-input
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/hooks/use-command-autocomplete.ts. This hook manages the /command autocomplete in chat input. (1) Hook state: isOpen (boolean), items (filtered WorkflowCommand[]), selectedIndex (number). (2) Hook params: inputValue (string), commands (WorkflowCommand[]). (3) Logic: when inputValue starts with '/', filter commands by matching the rest of the input against command.command and command.label. Open the dropdown. Arrow up/down changes selectedIndex. Enter selects the current item. Escape closes. (4) Returns: isOpen, filteredItems, selectedIndex, selectItem(index), close(), and keyboard handler props for the input. (5) Wire into chat-input.tsx: render a .wf-command-autocomplete dropdown above the input when isOpen. Each item shows command (mono bold violet), label, description. Use shadcn Command component or custom dropdown. (6) Selecting a command: inserts the command text into the input. If command's template expects entity arg, append a placeholder. VERIFICATION: Type '/' in chat input — dropdown shows 5 commands. Type '/r' — filters to /rent-roll. Arrow keys navigate. Enter selects. Escape closes. Dark mode works.

userStories.title: Create login.tsx + verify all route transitions work
userStories.description: This task has two parts. PART A — Create routes/login.tsx: A standalone login page (outside the _app layout). Read the existing login.html for reference. Structure: centered container with LogoFull component (6 large spheres), 'COSIMO' title in ChicagoFLF, 'MEDICI & COMPANY' subtitle, tagline text, SSO login button (shadcn Button, full width), and 'Powered by Medici Intelligence' footer. Dark background for the page (use Tailwind bg-slate-950 or similar). The login page does NOT use the app shell layout — it's a separate route. PART B — Route verification: Navigate to EVERY route and verify: (1) / redirects to /chat. (2) /chat shows thread list + placeholder or first thread. (3) /chat/fund3 shows Fund III messages. (4) /chat/nonexistent shows error boundary. (5) /workflows shows 7 template cards. (6) /workflows/rent-roll shows template detail. (7) /workflows/nonexistent shows error boundary. (8) /brain/memory shows memory facts. (9) /brain/lessons shows lesson cards. (10) /brain/lessons/{id} shows lesson detail. (11) /brain/graph shows entity graph. (12) /login shows login page. (13) All back buttons work. (14) Browser back/forward works. Fix any routing issues found. VERIFICATION: Every route path resolves correctly. No 404s. Login page renders with logo.

userStories.title: Create use-keyboard.ts + use-resize-panel.ts hooks
userStories.description: Create two hooks: (1) medici-app/app/hooks/use-keyboard.ts — Global keyboard shortcut handler. Uses useEffect to add a document keydown listener. Handles: Escape key with priority chain (close popover → close autocomplete → close Cosimo panel → close file/workflow panel → close header panels). Reads state from stores to determine which is open. Also handles: Cmd/Ctrl+K for search focus (if we add search). Clean up listener on unmount. (2) medici-app/app/hooks/use-resize-panel.ts — Drag-to-resize logic for the file panel and workflow panel. Takes: initialWidth, minWidth, maxWidth, side ('left'|'right'). Returns: currentWidth, isDragging, handleMouseDown (for the resize handle). Uses mousedown/mousemove/mouseup pattern. Stores width in state. (3) Wire use-keyboard.ts into the _app.tsx layout (call the hook once at the app shell level). (4) Wire use-resize-panel.ts into file-panel.tsx and workflow-panel.tsx. VERIFICATION: Escape key chain works (test by opening Cosimo panel → Escape closes it). Panel resize works by dragging the handle. No memory leaks (listeners cleaned up).

userStories.title: Create a11y.css + verify accessibility modes
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/a11y.css (imported in app.css). Add CSS overrides that Tailwind alone can't handle: (1) [data-a11y-font='dyslexia'] * { font-family: 'OpenDyslexic', sans-serif !important; } — include @font-face for OpenDyslexic if available, or use a Google Fonts alternative dyslexia-friendly font. (2) [data-a11y-motion='reduce'] * { animation-duration: 0.001ms !important; transition-duration: 0.001ms !important; } — this kills ALL animations when reduced motion is on. (3) [data-a11y-contrast='high'] { --border: 0 0% 0%; --muted-foreground: 0 0% 20%; } and .dark[data-a11y-contrast='high'] { --border: 0 0% 100%; --muted-foreground: 0 0% 80%; } — high contrast mode. (4) Verify: toggle each a11y mode via the profile dropdown and check that the entire app responds. Dyslexia font changes all text. Reduced motion stops all animations (including flow graph pulse, typing indicator). High contrast increases border/text contrast. (5) Font size slider: verify all 5 zoom levels don't break layout. VERIFICATION: Each a11y toggle works across all views. Reduced motion actually stops animations. High contrast makes borders/text more visible. Font sizes scale without layout breaks.

userStories.title: Create use-theme.ts hook — purple intensity + dark mode application
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/hooks/use-theme.ts. This hook applies theme state to the DOM: (1) Read all theme values from useThemeStore. (2) useEffect for dark mode: add/remove 'dark' class on document.documentElement. (3) useEffect for purple intensity: read CONFIG_PURPLE_BASE_COLORS (from config.ts), call adjustPurpleIntensity (from color-utils.ts), apply resulting CSS variables to document.documentElement.style. This adjusts all violet/purple shades based on the slider value. (4) useEffect for font size: set document.documentElement.style.fontSize based on fontSizeLevel and CONFIG.fontZoomLevels. (5) useEffect for data attributes: set/remove data-a11y-font, data-a11y-motion, data-a11y-contrast on document.documentElement. (6) Call this hook in root.tsx or _app.tsx so it runs once at the top level. VERIFICATION: Toggle dark mode — .dark class toggles on <html>. Move purple slider — inspect CSS variables, violet shades shift. Toggle a11y modes — data attributes appear on <html>. All effects persist across page reload (localStorage).

userStories.title: Dark mode verification pass — fix all dark mode issues
userStories.description: This is a VERIFICATION TASK. Systematically check every view in dark mode: (1) Set theme to 'dark'. (2) /chat: sidebar background, thread items, active thread highlight, message blocks, user/AI cards, artifacts, data tables, command chips, gate messages, chat input, file chips, attach menu, model selector. (3) /workflows: library cards, stats bar, template detail header, flow graph nodes/edges (all 5 node types), tabs, tab content, node popover. (4) /brain/memory: fact cards, category pills, entity chips, trait badges, search input, toolbar. (5) /brain/lessons: lesson cards, scope badges, detail view, edit mode. (6) /brain/graph: graph nodes (all types), edges, labels, entity detail panel. (7) Header: tabs, icons, profile dropdown, header panels (tasks, calendar, usage). (8) Cosimo panel. (9) Login page. (10) Check for: invisible text, missing borders, wrong backgrounds, low contrast text, broken hover states. Fix ALL issues found. VERIFICATION: Every view is legible and visually correct in dark mode. No invisible or unreadable elements.

userStories.title: Add HydrateFallback loading skeletons to all route files
userStories.description: Add loading states to every route file that has a loader. Use shadcn Skeleton component. (1) _app.chat.tsx: export HydrateFallback showing a list of 6 skeleton thread items in sidebar. (2) _app.chat.$threadId.tsx: HydrateFallback with 3 skeleton message blocks of varying heights. (3) _app.workflows.tsx: HydrateFallback with grid of 6 skeleton cards. (4) _app.workflows.$id.tsx: HydrateFallback with two-column layout: skeleton graph area + skeleton tab content. (5) _app.brain.memory.tsx: HydrateFallback with skeleton trait section + 5 skeleton fact cards. (6) _app.brain.lessons.tsx: HydrateFallback with 4 skeleton lesson cards. (7) _app.brain.lessons.$id.tsx: HydrateFallback with skeleton header + skeleton content block. (8) _app.brain.graph.tsx: HydrateFallback with skeleton graph area + skeleton detail panel. Each skeleton matches the approximate shape and size of the real content. VERIFICATION: Temporarily add a delay to a loader (e.g., await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 2000))) — skeletons should be visible during the delay. Remove delay after verification.

userStories.title: Add ErrorBoundary to all route files
userStories.description: Add error handling to every route file. Use shadcn Alert with variant='destructive'. (1) For each route file with a loader, add an ErrorBoundary export: shows an Alert with the error message and a 'Try again' button that reloads the route (via useRevalidator or link to same URL). (2) _app.chat.$threadId.tsx: 'Thread not found' for 404, generic error for others. (3) _app.workflows.$id.tsx: 'Template not found' for 404. (4) _app.brain.lessons.$id.tsx: 'Lesson not found' for 404. (5) _app.tsx: catch-all ErrorBoundary that shows a full-page error state with app logo and reload button. (6) Each error boundary should log the error to console for debugging. VERIFICATION: Navigate to /chat/nonexistent — shows thread not found error. Navigate to /workflows/nonexistent — shows template not found. Errors don't crash the app — they're caught by boundaries.

userStories.title: Create message-stream.tsx — Erabor streaming animation
userStories.description: Create medici-app/app/components/chat/message-stream.tsx. This recreates the Erabor thread's multi-phase streaming animation. Read app.js Chat.runEraborSequence, Chat.streamReply, Chat.typeTextBlock, Chat.streamSectionBlock carefully. (1) Create a useStream hook (or extend the component) that manages: Phase 1 'Thinking' (animated dots, typing indicator), Phase 2 sections stream in one at a time with typewriter effect, Phase 3 final content appears with any artifacts. (2) The component accepts the full message content and timing config. (3) On mount (or trigger), it runs through the phases with timeouts. (4) Respects [data-a11y-motion='reduce'] — in reduced motion mode, content appears instantly without animation. (5) Shows typing indicator dots as spheres (matching logo spheres — radial gradient, inset shadow, border-radius: 50%). (6) Wire into the Erabor thread message rendering — when the thread is selected and has a streaming message, use this component. VERIFICATION: Select Erabor thread — streaming animation plays. Typing dots are spheres. Content appears in sections. Reduced motion mode: content appears instantly.

userStories.title: Cross-view navigation verification and wiring
userStories.description: Verify and fix ALL cross-view navigation paths: (1) Library → Detail: click workflow card → navigates to /workflows/{id} → detail view loads. (2) Detail → Chat: click 'Run' button → navigates to /chat, selects mock run thread, opens workflow panel. Click run in Runs tab with threadId → navigates to /chat/{threadId}. (3) Chat → Workflows: (for later, ensure URL works). (4) Sidebar active run click → navigates to /chat/{threadId}, opens workflow panel. (5) Brain entity chip click in memory → navigates to /brain/graph?entity={name}. (6) Brain lesson linked workflow chip → navigates to /workflows/{id}. (7) Workflow detail 'Edit with Cosimo' → opens Cosimo panel with template context. (8) Node popover 'Edit with Cosimo' → opens Cosimo panel with node context. (9) Thread list workflow-linked thread click → auto-opens workflow panel. (10) Back buttons all work correctly (Library back, Lesson back, Brain section nav). (11) Toast notifications fire when navigating to completed run or waiting run. VERIFICATION: Every navigation path listed above works without errors. No dead-end states.

userStories.title: Final TypeScript compilation + lint check
userStories.description: This is a VERIFICATION TASK. Run comprehensive checks: (1) `npx tsc --noEmit` — fix ALL type errors. Common issues: untyped props, missing return types, incorrect generic parameters, unused imports. (2) Check for any remaining TODO comments in component files — each TODO should either be implemented or documented as intentional (for future API integration). (3) Verify no direct MOCK_* imports in component files — components should only import from services/ or receive data as props/loader data. (4) Verify no document.getElementById or document.querySelector calls in React components (except in hooks where necessary). (5) Verify no inline styles except for dynamic values (purple intensity, panel width, font size). (6) Check all files have proper TypeScript types — no `any` type usage. VERIFICATION: `npx tsc --noEmit` exits 0. Grep for 'any' type usage returns only intentional cases. Grep for 'document.get' returns only hook files.

userStories.title: Create docs/api-contract.md — API integration documentation
userStories.description: Create medici-app/docs/api-contract.md documenting every service function and its expected API mapping. For each service function: (1) Function signature with types. (2) Suggested REST endpoint (e.g., GET /api/threads → getThreads()). (3) Request parameters. (4) Response shape (reference TypeScript interface). (5) Current implementation (returns mock data). (6) Migration notes (what the backend team needs to implement). Cover all services: threads (getThreads, getThread, getThreadOrder), workflows (getTemplates, getTemplate, getRun, getRunsForTemplate, getCommands), brain (getMemory, getLessons, getLesson, getGraphData), panels (getTasks, getCalendar, getUsage, getSpreadsheet). VERIFICATION: Document covers all 15+ service functions. Each entry has endpoint, params, response type, and migration notes.

userStories.title: Create docs/component-map.md — shadcn component inventory + styling handoff
userStories.description: Create medici-app/docs/component-map.md with two sections: (1) Component Inventory: list every shadcn component used and which files use it. E.g., 'Button → workflow-card.tsx (Run), chat-input.tsx (Send), app-header.tsx (icons), cosimo-panel.tsx (Send)'. Cover: Button, Card, Tabs, Badge, Sheet, Table, DropdownMenu, Command, Popover, Switch, Slider, Skeleton, Alert, Dialog, Avatar, Separator, ScrollArea, Tooltip, Input, Label, Sidebar. (2) Styling Handoff: document which components use base shadcn styling (ready for CTO's diff) vs. custom styling. Base shadcn: all standard components. Custom: flow graph SVG, entity graph SVG, 6-sphere logo, streaming animation, gate message border, command chip. (3) Design Token Mapping: table showing how each original tokens.css variable maps to the shadcn CSS variable in app.css. VERIFICATION: Document is comprehensive and accurate. Every shadcn component is accounted for. Token mapping covers all variables.

userStories.title: Final QA — complete verification checklist
userStories.description: This is the FINAL VERIFICATION TASK. Run through the complete checklist from the refactoring plan: (1) `npm run dev` starts without errors. (2) All routes resolve correctly — test every URL path. (3) Dark mode toggle works — all components respond. (4) Purple intensity slider adjusts CSS variables in both themes. (5) All 8 chat threads render with correct messages. (6) Workflow library shows all 7 template cards. (7) Flow graph SVG renders for each template — test all 7. (8) Brain memory, lessons, and graph all load with correct data. (9) All accessibility toggles function (dyslexia font, reduced motion, high contrast, font size). (10) Sidebar collapses/expands with state persistence. (11) File panel and workflow context panel open correctly. (12) Cosimo panel slides in from all entry points. (13) Command autocomplete appears in chat input when typing /. (14) No direct MOCK_* imports in components. (15) No document.getElementById calls in components. (16) No inline styles except dynamic values. (17) `npx tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors. (18) No console errors in browser. (19) All cross-view navigation paths work. (20) Loading skeletons show during data loading. (21) Error boundaries catch route errors. Fix EVERYTHING found. This is the last task — the codebase must be production-ready.
